Title: Balancing the double-edged sword effect of increased resistant starch content and its impact on rice texture: its genetics and molecular physiological mechanisms

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Indica diversity panel; contrasting MRS and LRS indica lines
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Lowers glycemic index and supports gut health as fermentable substrate for probiotic bacteria
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed positive regulatory influence on resistant starch formation through starch-metabolism network control
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as one of the two hub genes with maximal interaction in the turquoise WGCNA module derived from DEGs between medium-RS and low-RS rice lines. 2. The network placement suggests it may regulate resistant starch through interactions with starch biosynthesis and degradation genes. 3. Highlighted by the authors as an important regulator alongside multiple starch metabolism genes.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Identified from developing seeds at 16 DAF in contrasting medium-RS and low-RS lines; differential-expression direction not explicitly stated in the main text
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Standard field growth; comparison between medium resistant starch and low resistant starch indica lines
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Microarray differential expression in developing seeds; WGCNA co-expression network analysis
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Resequencing (~2.2 million SNPs); microarray transcriptomics; metabolomics (GC-TOF)
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Causality is inferred from hub status in co-expression network, not from transgenic or biochemical validation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulatory target for modulating resistant starch with systems-level effects, but requires direct functional validation before deployment.

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Indica diversity panel; contrasting MRS and LRS indica lines
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Lowers glycemic index and supports gut health
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed regulator associated with resistant starch phenotype
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as a central hub gene in the turquoise co-expression module. 2. Also showed significant SNP association with resistant starch phenotype in TGAS. 3. Authors propose it as a potential key regulator of resistant starch formation despite unknown molecular function.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing seeds at 16 DAF; hub gene in DEG-derived network between MRS and LRS lines
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Standard field growth; comparison between medium resistant starch and low resistant starch indica lines
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Microarray differential expression; WGCNA network analysis; targeted gene association study
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unknown biochemical function; evidence is association plus network centrality rather than direct mechanistic proof.
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Protein phosphatase LOC_Os05g49730; starch-metabolism network genes (network-based)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate novel locus for marker-assisted selection or functional genomics discovery, but not yet ready for direct engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Resequenced indica panel; contrasting MRS and LRS indica lines
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improves gut health and lowers glycemic index
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream chain-elongation step affecting amylopectin structure
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: ADP-glucose; short A and B1 glucan chains of amylopectin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from ADP-glucose to elongate short amylopectin chains into longer chains, increasing chain length and starch crystallinity
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Diverts/structures flux toward enzyme-resistant starch by producing longer glucan chains that enhance crystallinity and digestional resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Superior SSIIa haplotype increases resistant starch and gelatinization temperature
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWAS and targeted association identified SSIIa as the primary locus for resistant starch and gelatinization temperature on chromosome 6. 2. Superior GGC haplotype was associated with moderate RS (~1.31%) and higher GT (~77.1 °C), whereas ATT haplotype conferred lower RS (~0.85%) and lower GT (~71.28 °C). 3. Functional SNPs included promoter and exon 8 variants, including a non-synonymous Leu→Phe substitution. 4. SSIIa explained 15.88% of RS phenotypic variation and was considered the major contributor among identified loci. 5. Expression of SSIIa was down-regulated in MRS lines, consistent with altered amylopectin biosynthesis patterns in lines carrying favorable haplotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Down-regulated in developing seeds (16 DAF) of MRS lines relative to LRS lines
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing seeds at 16 DAF; field-grown indica lines across wet and dry seasons
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; targeted gene association; haplotype analysis; differential expression analysis; starch structural analysis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Resequencing (~2.2 million SNPs); microarray transcriptomics; starch structure SEC
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for amylopectin chain elongation and major determinant of RS/GT variation in this study
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors note SSIIa high catalytic activity promotes higher crystallinity and melting temperature, thereby increasing digestion resistance. Despite lower transcript abundance in MRS, favorable alleles likely alter enzyme activity/structure.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Associated with higher gelatinization temperature and altered texture/hardness traits when RS increases
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value major marker for breeding moderate-RS rice with acceptable texture; useful for allele mining and marker-assisted selection, likely in combination with minor QTLs.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Resequenced indica panel; contrasting MRS and LRS indica lines
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improves gut health and lowers glycemic index
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Glycolysis / carbon partitioning linked to starch metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Plastidic sugar phosphorylation / carbon flux control
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Phosphorylates a six-carbon sugar, described by the authors as converting fructose to fructose-6-phosphate, thereby influencing sugar availability and carbon partitioning
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool and controls carbon flux between starch synthesis and non-starch polysaccharide metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Superior haplotypes are associated with increased resistant starch and higher gelatinization temperature
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Fine-mapped targeted association identified LOC_Os06g12600 pfkB as the second key gene near SSIIa on chromosome 6 associated with RS. 2. Nine significant SNPs defined superior haplotypes associated with mean RS ~1.28% and GT ~77.0 °C, versus inferior haplotype associated with lower RS (~0.84%) and lower GT (~71.72 °C). 3. Additive effects of SSIIa and pfkB together explained 19% of phenotypic variation for RS. 4. Authors propose plastidic pfkB as a regulator of carbon flux deciding between starch synthesis and non-starch polysaccharide synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Association-supported; specific DEG direction for this locus not explicitly stated in main text
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS fine mapping; targeted gene association; haplotype analysis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Proposed key carbon-flux control step, not formally established as rate-limiting enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper uses 'pfkB' nomenclature and discusses fructose phosphorylation/carbon partitioning; exact biochemical annotation within pfkB family is not rigorously resolved in the article.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altering central carbon flux may affect non-starch polysaccharides, sugars, amino acids and texture-related traits
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful minor-to-intermediate effect locus to stack with SSIIa and other starch genes for moderate-RS breeding.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Resequenced indica panel; contrasting MRS and LRS indica lines
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improves gut health and lowers glycemic index
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Amylose biosynthesis / extra-long amylopectin chain synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adds glucose units from ADP-glucose to form amylose and possibly extra-long amylopectin chains that contribute to digestion-resistant starch fractions
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms structural glucan components contributing to resistant starch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive alleles and higher expression are associated with increased resistant starch
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Twenty-four SNPs in GBSSI/Wx1 showed significant positive association with RS, with beta values ranging 0.22–0.39. 2. Variants were mainly in promoter, intron, 5'UTR and downstream regions, indicating regulatory polymorphism. 3. GBSSI expression remained higher in MRS than LRS lines and the authors describe it as the rate-limiting amylose biosynthesis gene. 4. The study supports a contribution of GBSSI to AM2/extra-long glucan chains that elevate RS.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in developing seeds (16 DAF) of MRS lines than LRS lines
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Targeted association analysis; differential expression analysis; starch structural interpretation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Resequencing (~2.2 million SNPs); microarray transcriptomics; starch structure SEC
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Described in the paper as the rate-limiting amylose biosynthesis gene
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as Wxa-related and previously implicated in RS by overexpression studies; in this paper evidence is natural allelic variation and expression correlation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher amylose/RS can increase cooked grain hardness and alter texture
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate to combine with SSIIa and other loci to raise RS while monitoring texture and amylose-related eating quality.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improves gut health and lowers glycemic index
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Precursor supply to starch and cell wall metabolism
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for downstream carbohydrate partitioning affecting resistant starch accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A SNP in LOC_Os02g58480 encoding sucrose synthase showed positive association with resistant starch. 2. Authors discuss sucrose synthase as part of carbon partitioning connected to pfkB-mediated sugar flux. 3. It contributes as a minor-effect locus in the polygenic RS architecture.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned also in network as RSUS2 among interacting starch-related genes.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May affect broader carbon allocation to cellulose/non-starch polysaccharides
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Minor QTL useful for stacking with major RS loci.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Resequenced indica panel; contrasting MRS and LRS lines
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improves gut health and lowers glycemic index
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Degradative side-route competing with starch accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes internal alpha-1,4-glucosidic linkages in starch polymers, promoting starch breakdown
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with target-product accumulation by degrading starch that could otherwise contribute to resistant starch fraction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative association with RS; lower expression in MRS supports reduced starch degradation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOC_Os08g36900 alpha amylase carried SNPs negatively associated with RS. 2. Alpha-amylase genes were down-regulated in MRS lines relative to LRS lines. 3. Combined effects of superior SSIIa and low-amylase haplotypes improved explained variance for RS to ~19.98%.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Down-regulated in developing seeds of MRS lines
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Targeted association analysis; differential expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts oppositely to RS accumulation as a degradative enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing amylase could affect starch mobilization or seed physiology, though not tested here
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful negative target for allele selection/editing to modestly increase RS when combined with biosynthetic loci.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Resequenced indica panel; contrasting MRS and LRS lines
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improves gut health and lowers glycemic index
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes alpha-1,4-glucans in starch, reducing accumulation of digestion-resistant starch structures
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative association with RS; reduced expression in MRS likely favors RS accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOC_Os08g36910 (AMY3D) showed negative SNP association with RS. 2. Alpha-amylase genes including AMY3D were down-regulated in MRS lines. 3. AMY3D appeared as a starch-metabolism node connected in the turquoise co-expression module.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Down-regulated in developing seeds of MRS lines
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Targeted association analysis; differential expression analysis; network analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents a degradative component whose reduction is associated with higher RS.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible effects on starch turnover/seed physiology not assessed
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Negative allele target for stacking with SSIIa and GBSSI favorable alleles.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Contrasting MRS and LRS indica lines; indica diversity panel
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Lowers glycemic index and promotes gut-health benefits
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Regulation of starch biosynthesis and degradation gene network affecting resistant starch formation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely negative regulators or modulators of RS, because several bHLH genes were down-regulated in MRS lines and showed strong network connectivity to starch-metabolism genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Several bHLH transcription factors were down-regulated in MRS lines. 2. TGAS identified bHLH loci associated with RS. 3. Network analysis showed high connectivity of bHLH factors with starch metabolism genes including SSIIa, GBSS, RSUS2, OsAGPS2, AMY3D and AMY3A. 4. Authors propose LOC_Os09g28210 and LOC_Os10g40740 as potential major regulators of RS biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence is correlative/network-based; no direct transactivation assays were reported. The paper treats bHLH regulators as important novel candidates.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Potential high-level regulators linking amylose/amylopectin ratio and starch metabolism to RS formation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Differential expression analysis; WGCNA co-expression network; targeted gene association
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising targets for regulatory engineering of moderate RS, but require direct functional validation to avoid pleiotropic effects.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible broad pleiotropic effects on grain traits and grain size, as bHLHs were previously implicated in starch ratio and grain size regulation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Down-regulated in developing seeds (16 DAF) of MRS lines
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Connected to SSIIa; GBSS; RSUS2; OsAGPS2; AMY3D; AMY3A (network-based)
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: SSIIa; GBSS; RSUS2; OsAGPS2; AMY3D; AMY3A
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SSIIa; GBSS; RSUS2; OsAGPS2; AMY3D; AMY3A
